sentence_reviews.text: GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and other AI accelerators execute dense, sparse, tensor, or event-driven operations with specialized memory hierarchies and interconnects.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: TPU and CUDA references directly support specialized accelerator hardware, memory hierarchy, parallel execution, and tensor-oriented computation; event-driven NPUs are broader than the cited sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dense/tensor accelerator parts are well supported; sparse and event-driven operation are plausible but not directly covered by the cited references.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Numerical analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, GPUs, TPUs & AI accelerators is modeled as depending on Linear algebra, Numerical analysis and feeding downstream nodes such as CUDA, ROCm, Triton & custom kernels, Quantized inference & compressed deployment, Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
